calculated_metrics.confidence_note: Cost per outcome calculation is not available. This organisation conducts significant multi-domain work (e.g., human education, environmental conservation). Dividing the total budget solely by quantified animal outcomes would artificially inflate the cost and misrepresent their financial efficiency.

details.formula: Calculation aborted due to multi-domain operations.

calculated_metrics.name: Cost Per Outcome (USD)

calculated_metrics.confidence_note: IES is a Back-of-the-Envelope Calculation (BOTEC) to standardize impact across interventions and species. It relies on philosophical and empirical assumptions.

details.criteria: Pass: <= 2 years of expenditure. | Warn: 2-5 years. | Fail: > 5 years.
Pass: >= 6 months of operating expenses. | Warn: 3-6 months. | Fail: < 3 months.
Pass: At least one intervention is classified as Tier 1 (Systemic Change) or Tier 2 (Prevention & Scalable Care). | Warn: All verifiable interventions are Tier 3 (Direct Care & Indirect Action).

details.formula: Mapping of verified intervention types to the Intervention Leverage Tier framework.

details.calculation: No valid counterfactual baseline was provided.
details.criteria: Pass: At least one metric has a quantified counterfactual baseline. | Warn: A qualitative baseline is provided, but lacks quantified data. | Fail: No valid counterfactual baseline is provided.
details.formula: Presence of a quantified counterfactual baseline with a source quote.

details.criteria: Pass: Government grants are < 80% of total income. | Fail: Government grants are >= 80% of total income.

details.calculation: Operates in a low-neglectedness / saturated area (Companion Animals: 37%, Unspecified: 63%).
details.criteria: Pass: >= 50% of beneficiaries are high-neglectedness (farmed/wild). | Warn: 1-49% are high-neglectedness. | Fail: 0% are high-neglectedness.
details.formula: Proportional analysis of beneficiary populations (farmed/wild vs. companion)

details.calculation: Organisation self-reported on unintended negative impacts.
details.criteria: Bonus: Organisation self-reported on unintended negative impacts. | Not Disclosed: No disclosure found.
details.elaboration: Quote: 'The SPCA has seen an increase in adopted puppies being returned as they grow older, with a key factor being the lack of proper training.'
details.formula: Check for self-reported unintended consequences or failures.

details.criteria: Bonus: Euthanasia/live-release data is disclosed. | Not Disclosed: No disclosure found. | N/A: Not applicable for non-sheltering organisations.

source.quote: This year, the SPCA has helped 4,731 animals through our core services.
907 pets from low-income households received vet care
Lost animals reunited with owner/claimed / released 176 276 7 459
Lost animals reunited with owner/claimed / released 176
Lost animals reunited with owner/claimed / released 176 276
Lost animals reunited with owner/claimed / released 176 276 7

SERVICES
• 24/7 emergency animal rescues
• Animal cruelty and welfare investigations
• Shelter for over 200 sick, injured, abused, abandoned,
and unwanted pets and community animals every
month
• Singapore’s only not-for-profit Community Animal
Clinic
• Adoption programme (includes the Project ADORE
scheme for Singapore Special dogs) and foster care
for animals in need
• Special operations to provide relief to guardians who
can no longer care for their pets
• Lost and found services for pets
• Education and outreach programmes
• Advocacy for pertinent animal welfare issues
• Management of the nationwide, government-funded
Trap-Neuter-Release-Manage (TNRM) programme for
dogs
This year, the SPCA has helped 4,731 animals through our core services.
Lost animals reunited with owner/claimed / released 176 276 7 459
907 pets from low-income households received vet care
Our Community Animal Clinic, the only one of its kind in Singapore, performed 788 surgeries
we organised 7 health screenings, assisting 667 animals.
the SPCA attended to a hoarding case involving 51 hamsters kept in a flat at MacPherson.
We engaged 9,680 young people through our education programmes
our growing group of 342 Youth Ambassadors has participated in diverse and interactive programmes
In the past year, our staff put in 1,218 hours of training.
27 organisations have come on board for corporate volunteering this year.
10 successful groups were awarded $1,000 grants each
